learning_objectives: Classify whole numbers as odd, even, prime and composite in different situations
bullet_points: Sort numbers (1–100) into odd and even, then look for patterns.
From the sorted list, identify even and odd numbers (explain why the number 2 is special).
Discuss how a number is classified as even or odd.
Brainstorm real-life examples where these number classifications are found.

learning_objectives: Classify real numbers as rational and irrational in different situations
bullet_points: Choose any pair of natural numbers between 1 and 10 and create at least two fractions: one proper and one improper.
Use a calculator to divide each fraction and record the decimal value.
Choose any set of natural numbers between 1 and 20 and write each number as a square root.
Classify fractions, decimals, and square roots as either rational or irrational.
Discuss how a number is classified as rational or irrational.
Brainstorm real-life examples where these number classifications are found.
